objectives: Full security audit of MX20022 payments runtime workspace
Cover OWASP Top 10, crypto, auth, input validation, CI supply chain
constraints: Rust workspace, no unsafe blocks found
important_facts: CRITICAL: HKDF salt is hardcoded static string 'mx20022-v1' in crypto lib
CRITICAL: CryptoService derives Clone, key_bytes copied in memory on clone
HIGH: Legacy bearer auth accepts ANY non-empty token as valid for most resources
HIGH: Admin auth defaults to LegacyBearer mode (AuthConfig::default), config defaults to disabled
HIGH: No CORS policy configured on any HTTP endpoint
HIGH: No security headers (HSTS, CSP, X-Frame-Options, etc.)
HIGH: TCP channel has no authentication at all
HIGH: HTTP channel uses from_utf8_lossy - silently replaces invalid UTF-8
MEDIUM: Store URL (may contain credentials) stored in struct and exposed via database_url() getter
MEDIUM: jwt_hs256_secret stored as plaintext String in config struct in memory
MEDIUM: CI uses cargo install cargo-deny without pinned version
MEDIUM: deny.toml sources.unknown-registry and unknown-git are only warn, not deny
LOW: No rate limiting on admin endpoints
LOW: Config file hash uses DefaultHasher (not cryptographic)
risks_issues: Legacy bearer mode is a significant auth bypass risk in production
TCP channel accepts raw connections with no auth - suitable only for trusted networks
Static HKDF salt reduces entropy of derived keys

iteration.summary: Completed full security audit of all crates in the MX20022 workspace. Reviewed crypto (AES-256-GCM with HKDF), admin auth (JWT HS256 + legacy bearer + mTLS), HTTP/TCP/gRPC channels, SQLite/Postgres/RocksDB stores, runtime bootstrap, config parsing, and CI pipeline. Found 3 CRITICAL, 6 HIGH, 5 MEDIUM, and 3 LOW severity issues. No SQL injection risks found (parameterized queries throughout). No unsafe Rust blocks. No hardcoded credentials in non-test code.
next_actions: Add security headers middleware to admin HTTP host
Add auth to TCP channel or document network isolation requirement
Replace static HKDF salt with per-deployment configurable salt

mentions.relevance_note: Code quality findings may overlap with security input validation concerns
Test coverage gaps in auth and crypto paths are security-relevant
